perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a close-up of a person sitting on a sofa, using a white tablet with a stylus; a circular overlay shows the main character, Tim, speaking. (2) The scene transitions to a medium shot of Tim, wearing a brown hoodie, glasses, and a patterned flat cap, gesturing while speaking in a studio setting. (3) B-roll footage follows, showing a child reading a physical picture book, a close-up of someone writing a 'To Do' list on paper, and students in a classroom looking at smartphones. (4) Large, bold text overlays appear on screen in several segments to emphasize key points about digital distraction and the return to physical books. (5) The video ends with Tim pointing a finger and asking a question to the audience.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, medium-tempo electronic background music starts immediately. An adult male voice (Tim) begins speaking with an energetic, friendly, and professional tone. (2) The music continues at a consistent volume as the narrator discusses the trend of using 3C products in education and a counter-trend in a Finnish town. (3) No ambient noises or significant sound effects are present; the audio remains focused on the clear studio-recorded narration and the persistent BGM.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: Subtitles at the bottom of the screen translating the narrator's speech into Traditional Chinese. (2) Hook Text: '使用平板筆電做教學' (Using tablets and laptops for teaching) appears in large yellow and white font. (3) Decorative/Effect Text: '但會干擾學生的注意力' (But it will interfere with students' attention) and '容易造成專注力下降' (Easily causes focus to drop) appear in large white font with black outlines to highlight the risks discussed. (4) Brand Text: 'miko' logo in the top left and social media handles like '3c有意思tim哥' in the top right.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Hook -> Commentary on Global Trends -> Scientific/Social Evidence -> Conclusion/Question

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main creator (Tim), 1 child reading, 1 person writing, and at least 1 student visible in B-roll.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: An adult male with glasses, wearing a brown zippered hoodie and a grey patterned flat cap.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The main scene is a studio with soft, blurred background elements; B-roll scenes include a cozy home setting and a standard classroom environment.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: Linear: Hook -> Contrast (Finland's analog shift) -> Problem Definition (Distraction/Health) -> Alternative (Physical books) -> CTA/Question
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a social commentary on the impact of digital technology in education. It highlights a specific case in Finland where schools are returning to physical books and paper to combat the negative effects of screen time, such as reduced attention spans and sleep issues, ultimately asking the audience for their opinion on digital teaching.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The brand 'miko' is prominently and consistently displayed in the top left corner of the frame throughout the video, suggesting a sponsorship or partnership with the creator.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Narrator uses a circular portrait overlay over B-roll; consistent use of large bold Chinese characters for key emphasis.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The content leverages the influencer's 3C (Computer, Communication, Consumer Electronics) expertise to discuss a broader educational and social issue, making it highly suitable for clusters related to technology criticism or educational trends.
